attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: None required for presence/auditability; for maximal rigor, include explicit formula cells (spreadsheet formula strings) or a short code-cell reference line in the CSV metadata column to show exact computation steps used to derive each computed field.
rubric_item_scores.notes: CSV evidence table includes one row per embedded record (R1..R4), governing-limit fields, fit metadata (b0, b1), fitted values, residuals, residual tolerance rule (RMSE), sensitivity-shifted fitted values, direction/reversal flags, needs_review text, and record-level citation. Calculations visible in columns and appear reproducible from previewed model coefficients and rows.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: CSV evidence table includes one row per embedded record (R1..R4), governing-limit fields, fit metadata (b0, b1), fitted values, residuals, residual tolerance rule (RMSE), sensitivity-shifted fitted values, direction/reversal flags, needs_review text, and record-level citation. Calculations visible in columns and appear reproducible from previewed model coefficients and rows.

rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Economic allocation method is documented but numerical allocations in ops_brief do not match the documented 'severity' normalization step (inconsistency between stated severity weights and resulting USD allocations).
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Fix the budget-allocation arithmetic to match the documented severity normalization (show the intermediate normalized weights and calculation steps). Explicitly state whether sensitivity is applied to coefficients or predicted values (both are acceptable if justified) and show the small numeric check demonstrating residuals under shifted fits.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Ops brief documents model choice (simple linear), provides coefficient derivation, RMSE tolerance definition, and an executable logic snippet. Sensitivity analysis is present (+/-5%) and applied (coefficients scaled and residuals recomputed) and tied to the CSV. Economic impact calculation is present and references decision_budget_usd; overall methods and reproducible logic are included.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Ops brief documents model choice (simple linear), provides coefficient derivation, RMSE tolerance definition, and an executable logic snippet. Sensitivity analysis is present (+/-5%) and applied (coefficients scaled and residuals recomputed) and tied to the CSV. Economic impact calculation is present and references decision_budget_usd; overall methods and reproducible logic are included.

rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Per-record budget allocations do not match the stated severity-based normalization formula; this inconsistency undermines the reproducibility of the economic-priority allocation despite the total budget respecting the provided limit.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Recompute and show the allocation math step-by-step so each recommended USD amount is reproducible from the documented severity metric. Provide sensitivity on budget allocation (e.g., alternate allocations if R3 remains the only robust anomaly under +/-5%).
rubric_item_scores.notes: Recommendation table links specific anomaly signals (residual threshold exceedance, direction reversal) to records R2, R3, R4 with per-record justification and proposed actions. Budget allocations respect the decision_budget_usd total and at least one operational action is proposed per flagged anomaly.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Recommendation table links specific anomaly signals (residual threshold exceedance, direction reversal) to records R2, R3, R4 with per-record justification and proposed actions. Budget allocations respect the decision_budget_usd total and at least one operational action is proposed per flagged anomaly.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Expand the assumptions list to explicitly characterize any computational decisions (e.g., use of population RMSE vs sample RMSE) and mark consequential choices as needs_review where they materially affect anomaly identification thresholds.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Ops brief contains an assumptions section, explicitly states source is the embedded packet only, enumerates validation checks (row completeness, residual formula, RMSE threshold, monotone direction check, governance limits), and includes a needs_review column with record-level rationale for follow-up. Unresolved items are flagged appropriately.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Ops brief contains an assumptions section, explicitly states source is the embedded packet only, enumerates validation checks (row completeness, residual formula, RMSE threshold, monotone direction check, governance limits), and includes a needs_review column with record-level rationale for follow-up. Unresolved items are flagged appropriately.

This will remove residual uncertainty from trace-only assessment.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Trace shows creation of a CSV with 4 rows, fitted values, residuals, tolerance and sensitivity columns, and needs_review annotations. The worker computed OLS coefficients and per-record residuals in the trace and wrote them into [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Embed copy-pasteable runnable code blocks or spreadsheet formulas in ops_brief.md and include a short reproducibility checklist (commands to run) so verifiers can re-run the fit without relying on the trace alone.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Trace records a written ops_brief.md that includes methods, explicit formula (ais_hat = intercept + coef*berth_delay + coef*draught), sensitivity (5% shift) and economic proxy linking to decision_budget_usd. The pure-Python fallback solver was used and documented in the trace.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include explicit per-action budget line items summing to <= decision_budget_usd in the ops_brief.md and show the arithmetic (sum) in the artifact; attach a reconciliation table to make budget verification trivial.
